conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
When you feel like you can't do something simple like cooking, what goes through your mind about yourself?
I think I’m losing myself. I used to be the one who could handle anything, and now I’m just tired all the time. It’s hard to accept that I’m not the same person.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I’m disappearing. I look in the mirror, and I don't see the woman I used to be.
When you look in the mirror and don't see the woman you used to be, what do you see instead?
I see someone who looks tired and worn out. I don't recognize the person staring back, and it's hard to look at.
How do you see things going for you in the near future?
Honestly, I don't see it getting better. I just see more of this, day after day.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
About a year ago, maybe. It started when I realized my marriage was over and I was starting from scratch.
You mentioned your marriage ending—how has that affected your day-to-day emotions?
It makes everything feel hollow. I look around my house and realize I don't have anyone to share it with.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Just everything. I wake up, I exist, and then I go to bed. There’s no purpose to it anymore.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to get excited about your day when morning comes?
Yeah, it does. I just feel so tired, even when I wake up. It’s like I’m carrying this weight around that I can’t seem to put down.
With that tiredness and weight you mentioned, do you still find yourself wanting to do the things you usually enjoy?
No, not really. I used to love going for a run, but lately, even the thought of getting out of bed makes me feel so exhausted that I just can't bring myself to do it.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, how does that make you feel about yourself?
It makes me feel like I’m not good enough. Like I should be able to figure it out, but I just can’t seem to get my head on straight.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I’m stuck in this loop of regret. I keep thinking maybe if I had done this or said that, things would be different, but it doesn’t seem to be getting any better.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things could get better, or does it seem like this loop will just continue?
It feels like it will just keep going. I don't see how I can break the cycle unless I figure out what I did wrong, but I don't know how to do that.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It started a few weeks after the breakup. At first, it was just a few nights where I couldn't sleep, but now it feels like it's been months and I still can't shake it off.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It’s just constant. I walk into the office and I’m already tired, and then I have to go through the motions of work, but it all just feels like a chore.
You mentioned everything feels like a chore—has that made you feel more on edge or irritable with people around you?
Yeah, definitely. I get annoyed at little things that I wouldn't have noticed before. I just feel so drained that I don't have the energy to deal with other people.
